official.name: Jim Jordan
official.office_address: 2056 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3504

summary.narrative: Across his campaign-era commitments, Jordan repeatedly took formal legislative action — sponsoring pro-life, traditional-marriage, and spending-reduction bills, and earning a top fiscal-conservative ranking in 2009. However, the supplied record shows these sponsored measures were generally referred to committee, failed, or stalled, with multiple sources noting no bill he personally introduced has been signed into law. The education/parents commitment is supported mainly by position statements with no specific documented action.
summary.grade_rationale: The pattern is consistent introduction and advocacy (formal action initiated) without documented advancement to enactment on the cited measures, placing most promises at the early milestone-ladder rungs; promise clarity is largely directional-to-specific, and the official's causal role is dependent on a Congress and courts not fully aligned with him.

promises.text: Protect the sanctity of human life with an unwavering commitment as a pro-life legislator.

data_gaps: Campaign-window dating is thin (only 2 candidates window-dated); 2006 campaign-website promises (c1-c3) are Tier-2 researcher-supplied. The education promise has limited evidence. Many delivery-evidence sponsorships span multiple Congresses; the engine should note none cited here is shown signed into law (c113, c114, c158).

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 97.3% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
